---
title: "Week 3 starter (Python)"
subtitle: "Load the course dataset, confirm shape, render one summary table"
---

::: {.callout-important}
## What this is — and what it is not
This starter loads the GSE96870 cerebellum subset, confirms the dataset is what you think it is, and renders one summary table. **It does not perform the QC analysis.** That is the week 3 mini-project's *AI-free baseline* — you write that yourself, by hand, before you involve an AI assistant. See the [syllabus AI-use policy](../course-syllabus.qmd#ai-use-policy).

If you copy from this starter into your baseline, you are short-circuiting the assignment. The starter exists so you do not waste the first 30 minutes of the workshop on file paths.
:::

## Setup

You need Python (≥ 3.10) and `pandas`. Install with:

```bash
pip install pandas
```

This starter does not install `scanpy` or `pydeseq2` — those come later, when you write the baseline.

## Load the data from the Carpentries mirror

The two CSVs live in the [carpentries-incubator/bioc-rnaseq](https://github.com/carpentries-incubator/bioc-rnaseq) repository (CC-BY 4.0). They are stable URLs.

```python
import pandas as pd

BASE = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/carpentries-incubator/bioc-rnaseq/main/episodes/data"

coldata = pd.read_csv(f"{BASE}/GSE96870_coldata_cerebellum.csv")
counts  = pd.read_csv(f"{BASE}/GSE96870_counts_cerebellum.csv", index_col="gene")
```

## Confirm the shape

```python
print(counts.shape)     # expect (41786, 22)
print(coldata.shape)    # expect (22, 11)
assert list(coldata["sample"]) == list(counts.columns), "sample sheet does not match count columns"
```

If any of those three checks fails, **stop**. The downstream baseline will produce nonsense if the sample sheet does not align with the count columns.

## One summary table

```python
(coldata
   .groupby(["sex", "infection", "time"])
   .size()
   .reset_index(name="n")
   .sort_values(["infection", "time", "sex"]))
```

Read the output. There are 22 samples — *not* 24. Two of the twelve `sex × infection × time` cells are smaller than the others. This is a real feature of the dataset and a real source of imbalance the baseline analysis will need to be honest about.

That is the end of the starter. From here, the baseline is yours.

## Where to go next

- [Data analysis](../bioinformatics/data-analysis.qmd) — full worked-example walkthrough of QC on this dataset (in R; the Python equivalent is one-to-one for these steps).
- [Week 3 — Hands-on bioinformatics I](week-3.qmd) — the mini-project deliverable.
